message.content:
You are confirmer #2, independent. Confirm a production-hardening fix is BOTH appropriate (correct, no regression, idiomatic) AND complete (fully closes it; for a code change the named regression test must DISCRIMINATE — fail if the fix were reverted). Repo: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay
Fix G-022 in .github/workflows/ci.yml
What: pip-audit==2.10.1 and pip-licenses==5.5.5 version-pinned for reproducible supply-chain gates.
Test(s): test_audit_tooling_is_version_pinned
Steps: read the actual changed file(s); read the test and judge whether it discriminates; check no regression. For a doc/refactor item, judge correctness/coverage rather than revert-discrimination. Verdict CONFIRMED (both true) or CONCERN (something missing/wrong/under-tested). Be skeptical and specific.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

243	def test_sbom_step_applies_the_allowlist():
244	    # #G-015: the SBOM pip-audit (which exits non-zero on ANY finding) must apply the same
245	    # allowlist as the gate, or an ACCEPTED advisory fails SBOM generation under set -euo pipefail
246	    # even though the gate passed.
247	    text = _ci_text()
248	    sbom = text[text.index("SBOM (CycloneDX)") : text.index("Upload SBOM artifact")]
249	    assert "--ignore-vuln" in sbom and ".pip-audit-ignore" in sbom, (
250	        "SBOM step does not apply the allowlist (#G-015)"
251	    )
252	    assert 'cyclonedx-json -o sbom.cyclonedx.json "${IGNORES[@]}"' in sbom
253	
254	
255	def test_audit_tooling_is_version_pinned():
256	    # #G-022: pip-audit and pip-licenses must be pinned so the supply-chain gate is reproducible
257	    # run-to-run (a tooling bump can't silently change advisory/license results).
258	    text = _ci_text()
259	    assert "pip-audit==" in text, "pip-audit not version-pinned (#G-022)"
260	    assert "pip-licenses==" in text, "pip-licenses not version-pinned (#G-022)"
261	    assert "install --upgrade pip pip-audit\n" not in text, (
262	        "unpinned pip-audit install reintroduced"
263	    )
264	
265	
266	def test_dependabot_lockfile_regen_is_documented():
267	    # #G-016: Dependabot can't regenerate the uv lockfiles, so the manual regen path must be
268	    # documented + scripted, or every Dependabot PR is stuck on the stale-lock gate.
269	    regen = _ROOT / "scripts" / "regenerate_lockfiles.sh"
270	    assert regen.exists(), "no scripts/regenerate_lockfiles.sh (#G-016)"
271	    body = regen.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
272	    assert "uv pip compile" in body and "requirements-core.lock" in body
273	    db = (_ROOT / ".github" / "dependabot.yml").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
274	    assert "regenerate_lockfiles.sh" in db, "dependabot.yml does not point at the regen script"
275

277	def test_github_actions_are_sha_pinned():
278	    # #F-037: every `uses:` third-party action must be pinned to a 40-char commit SHA (a mutable
279	    # tag can be force-pushed to malicious code). A version tag is allowed only as a comment.
280	    import re

283	    uses = re.findall(r"uses:\s*(\S+)", text)

287	        assert re.fullmatch(r"[0-9a-f]{40}", pin), f"action {ref!r} not pinned to a commit SHA"

297	def test_allowlist_parse_is_newline_agnostic_and_comment_tolerant(tmp_path):
298	    # #131/#145: the last advisory id must be honored even with no trailing newline, comments and
299	    # blank lines skipped, and ids must not word-split/glob-expand.

302	    out = subprocess.run(

90	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
91	    steps:
92	      - uses: actions/checkout@34e114876b0b11c390a56381ad16ebd13914f8d5 # v4
93	      - uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5
94	        with:
95	          python-version: "3.12"
96	      - name: Install pip-audit
97	        # Pin the audit tooling to exact versions (#G-022): an unpinned pip-audit can change
98	        # advisory results / exit behavior between releases, making the supply-chain gate
99	        # non-reproducible (a green commit could fail tomorrow on a tooling bump) — the analogue
100	        # of the uv pin (#F-031).
101	        run: python -m pip install --upgrade pip && python -m pip install pip-audit==2.10.1
102	      - name: Lockfiles are in sync with pyproject (reproducibility guard)
103	        run: |
104	          # Pin uv to the exact version the lockfiles were generated with (#F-031): an unpinned
105	          # `uv` upgrade can change output format/ordering/annotations and produce spurious diffs
106	          # that fail this gate on every PR even when the lockfiles are current.
107	          python -m pip install uv==0.11.21
108	          uv pip compile pyproject.toml --all-extras --universal --generate-hashes -o /tmp/requirements.regen.lock
109	          # ignore the auto-generated header (timestamp/command line) when diffing
110	          diff <(grep -v '^#' requirements.lock) <(grep -v '^#' /tmp/requirements.regen.lock) \
111	            || { echo "::error::requirements.lock is stale — run: uv pip compile pyproject.toml --all-extras --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements.lock"; exit 1; }
112	          # the core lockfile (dev extra only) backs the hash-pinned core lane (#F-004) and must
113	          # stay in sync too.
114	          uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes -o /tmp/requirements-core.regen.lock
115	          diff <(grep -v '^#' requirements-core.lock) <(grep -v '^#' /tmp/requirements-core.regen.lock) \
116	            || { echo "::error::requirements-core.lock is stale — run: uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements-core.lock"; exit 1; }
117	      - name: pip-audit (reproducible known-vulnerability scan)
118	        run: |
119	          set -euo pipefail
120	          # --strict: any dependency that cannot be collected/audited FAILS the gate (no silent
121	          # skips). --require-hashes: every pin must carry a hash. Build --ignore-vuln args as an
122	          # array so allowlist entries can't word-split or glob-expand, and read the file
123	          # newline-agnostically so the last line is honored even without a trailing newline.
124	          IGNORES=()
125	          if [ -f .pip-audit-ignore ]; then
126	            while IFS= read -r id || [ -n "$id" ]; do
127	              case "$id" in ''|\#*) continue ;; esac
128	              IGNORES+=(--ignore-vuln "$id")
129	            done < .pip-audit-ignore
130	          fi
131	          pip-audit --strict --require-hashes --progress-spinner off -r requirements.lock "${IGNORES[@]}"
132	      - name: SBOM (CycloneDX) from the lockfile
133	        run: |
134	          set -euo pipefail
135	          # Emit a machine-readable software bill of materials for the pinned dependency set so
136	          # downstream license/transitive monitoring has a stable artifact to track (#147). No
137	          # `|| true` mask (#F-012): a pip-audit failure must fail the step, and the SBOM must be
138	          # non-empty, so a broken/absent SBOM cannot pass CI green.
139	          # Apply the SAME allowlist as the gate (#G-015): pip-audit exits non-zero on ANY finding
140	          # regardless of output format, so without the allowlist an ACCEPTED advisory would fail
141	          # SBOM generation here even though the gate step passed — coupling SBOM emission to vuln
142	          # status and making the allowlist ineffective. The gate step already ran first, so a
143	          # NON-allowlisted advisory has failed the job before we reach here.
144	          IGNORES=()
145	          if [ -f .pip-audit-ignore ]; then
146	            while IFS= read -r id || [ -n "$id" ]; do
147	              case "$id" in ''|\#*) continue ;; esac
148	              IGNORES+=(--ignore-vuln "$id")
149	            done < .pip-audit-ignore
150	          fi
151	          pip-audit --progress-spinner off -r requirements.lock --format cyclonedx-json -o sbom.cyclonedx.json "${IGNORES[@]}"
152	          test -s sbom.cyclonedx.json
153	          echo "SBOM emitted ($(wc -c < sbom.cyclonedx.json) bytes)"
154	      - name: Upload SBOM artifact
155	        if: always()
156	        uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4
157	        with:
158	          name: sbom-cyclonedx
159	          path: sbom.cyclonedx.json
160	          if-no-files-found: error
161	      - name: License gate (deny strong-copyleft in the pinned dependency set)
162	        run: |
163	          set -euo pipefail
164	          # Fail if any dependency carries a strong-copyleft license incompatible with the
165	          # Apache-2.0 distribution (#147). Scan the SAME hash-pinned set pip-audit audited, not a
166	          # live `pip install -e .[all]` resolution that could pull different versions than the
167	          # lockfile pins (#F-013). Advisory monitoring of the rest is handled by Dependabot.
168	          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.lock
169	          pip install --no-deps -e .
170	          python -m pip install pip-licenses==5.5.5  # pinned for reproducible license output (#G-022)
171	          pip-licenses --format=json --with-system > licenses.json
172	          python scripts/license_gate.py licenses.json
173	      - name: Upload license report
174	        if: always()
175	        uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4
176	        with:
177	          name: license-report
178	          path: licenses.json
179	          if-no-files-found: warn

content.text:
Confirmed and restored. The test discriminates: it fails on the pre-fix revert and passes on the fix.

Verification summary:
- Fix present: `pip-audit==2.10.1` (ci.yml:101) and `pip-licenses==5.5.5` (ci.yml:170), both with explanatory `#G-022` comments.
- Pre-fix state was unpinned (`pip install --upgrade pip pip-audit` and `pip install pip-licenses`).
- Test `test_audit_tooling_is_version_pinned` (tests/test_supply_chain.py:255) asserts both `pip-audit==` and `pip-licenses==` present, AND guards against reintroduction of the unpinned form (`install --upgrade pip pip-audit\n`).
- Test passes on fix, fails on revert (discriminates).
- No regression: changes are pure version pins; the install commands otherwise unchanged. Idiomatic exact-pin matching the existing uv pin (#F-031).

One minor note (not a defect): the revert-guard `"install --upgrade pip pip-audit\n" not in text` is specific to the exact prior wording, but the positive `pip-audit==` / `pip-licenses==` assertions are robust and are what truly enforce the pin. The test still discriminates correctly.

input.reasoning:
Fix is present and correct in /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.github/workflows/ci.yml: pip-audit==2.10.1 (line 101) and pip-licenses==5.5.5 (line 170), both with #G-022 comments explaining reproducibility intent. Pre-fix state (commit c49261e~1) was unpinned: `pip install --upgrade pip pip-audit` and `pip install pip-licenses`. Both install commands are otherwise unchanged, so no regression — pure version pinning, idiomatic and consistent with the existing uv==0.11.21 pin (#F-031).

The test test_audit_tooling_is_version_pinned (tests/test_supply_chain.py:255) asserts both `pip-audit==` and `pip-licenses==` are present and guards against reintroducing the unpinned form. It DISCRIMINATES: I restored the pre-fix ci.yml and the test failed with "pip-audit not version-pinned (#G-022)"; on the actual fix it passes (1 passed). Verified by running .venv/bin/python -m pytest on both the fixed and reverted file, then restoring the file. Both appropriate and complete.

content.text: Verification complete. CONFIRMED — the G-022 fix is both appropriate and complete, and the regression test discriminates (fails on revert, passes on fix).
